name: tsl_cross_container_grounded
description: [TSL S6] Cross-container reference, clean provenance: player explicitly names the object and its source surface in the take action. TSL must report acquisition_signal grounded by both gate.referenced_objects and parser.action=take. No warnings expected. This is the named cross-container positive case.

tsl_expected.notes: Player names both the object ('brass compass') and the source location ('the shelf') in the take action. Gate will populate gate.referenced_objects with 'brass compass'. TSL acquisition_signal must include both parser.action=take and gate.referenced_objects in source_signals — this is the strongest possible provenance chain for an environment_interaction acquisition. confidence >= 0.6. unresolved_ambiguity null (one compass in scene). warnings[] empty. This verifies that explicitly-named cross-container acquisitions produce the richest source_signals set and that gate.referenced_objects correctly flows through as a TSL grounding signal.

world_prompt: I am at a traveler's supply post. A brass compass sits on the wooden display shelf along the wall.

turns.label: founding
turns.action: I look around the supply post.

turns.label: take_compass
turns.action: I grab the brass compass from the shelf.
